narrative.summary:
📍 Stratus
TD South Tower, 79 Wellington St W tower 36th floor, Toronto, ON M5K 1J5, Canada

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You take the express elevator up 36 floors from the Financial District's TD South Tower, stepping out to floor-to-ceiling windows that frame the entire Toronto skyline. The dining room feels suspended—glass on three sides, tables spaced wide enough that the view becomes part of your table setting. At night, the city lights reflect off your wine glass before you even taste what's in it.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The kitchen leans into refined Canadian ingredients with the kind of precision you'd expect at this altitude—both literal and figurative. You're looking at dishes that might include seared scallops with brown butter or duck breast with seasonal accompaniments, executed with the polish that comes with a $50-80 per person dinner price point. The wine list matches the view: deep, expensive, worth it if you're celebrating.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Arrived late for a birthday Jan 28th reso before seeing a play. The host was gracious about seating us. Our server was amazing - can't remember his name but he was from out East. So friendly and lovely. Food was good and the best birthday treat was they dropped off 2 glasses of prosecco and a card signed by the staff. We felt so well treated and welcomed by all the staff. The view is amazing. You should all go."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Stratus is truly a standout high-end restaurant in Toronto. The service here is unlike any other; the staff go above and beyond, and the level of customer care is worth every penny. The experience and the incredible skyscraper views alone are enough to make me want to return.

As for the food, my experience was mixed. I ordered the albacore tuna bowl, and unfortunately the tuna didn’t feel very fresh and didn’t sit well with me afterward. However, my friend had the short rib and said it was amazing, so it may have just been an off plate for me that day.

Even so, I would absolutely go back for the exceptional service and to try something different. It is rare to find a place where the servers make you feel so welcome, and Stratus definitely did not miss in that regard. The atmosphere and views make it a memorable spot worth revisiting."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Came here for a group event and want to first point out the atmosphere, view, and services were exceptional. Beautiful city view especially during the sunset and evening. The food, however, was a bit more on the mediocre side. The salad and salmon lacked the flavors I was expecting. The braised short rib, though, was really good. At the start, it was a bit on the bland side, but with a little bit of salt the flavor were significantly elevated. The texture was amazing that it simply melts in your mouth. It was definitely the highlight dish of the evening. The crème brûlée was rather sour, but quickly acquired to be rather tasty. We had an open bar which was great, but the alcohol felt diluted. Overall, great experience and still a wonderful restaurant."
